info.name: Conversation Engine
info.description:
Production-ready FastAPI + OpenAI service with streaming, conversation history, and hexagonal architecture.

## Access points
- **`base_url`** (`http://localhost:8000`) — FastAPI directly. Use this for backend development and testing.
- **`frontend_base_url`** (`http://localhost:3000/api`) — Next.js BFF proxy. Use this to test the full stack as the browser would. All paths and schemas are identical.

Run `docker compose up` to start both services together.

## Quick start
1. Choose your `base_url` or `frontend_base_url` variable.
2. Run **Create Conversation** — the test script automatically saves `conversation_id`.
3. Run **Append Turn** to continue the conversation.

## SSE Streaming
Streaming endpoints return `text/event-stream`. Use Postman's "Send and Download" or a CLI like `curl -N` to observe events in real time. The terminal `done` event always closes the stream.

variable.description: FastAPI base URL — direct backend access
Next.js BFF base URL — same API as base_url, accessed through the frontend proxy
Set this after creating a conversation

request.description: Returns `{"status": "ok"}` when the service is running.
Returns conversations ordered by `created_at` DESC with page-based pagination.
Creates a new conversation and processes the first user turn synchronously. The test script automatically saves `conversation_id` as a collection variable.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
Creates a new conversation and streams the assistant reply as SSE.

Event order: `meta` → `chunk`* → `done`

Use "Send and Download" in Postman, or:
```
curl -N -X POST http://localhost:8000/conversations/stream \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Hello"}]}'
```

response.name: 200 OK — SSE stream

request.description:
Appends a new user turn to an existing conversation. History is automatically trimmed before the LLM call.

Requires `conversation_id` collection variable to be set (done automatically by the Create Conversation test script).

Appends a new user turn to an existing conversation and streams the reply as SSE.

Event order: `meta` → `chunk`* → `done`

Use "Send and Download" in Postman, or use curl with `-N`.

response.name: 200 OK — SSE stream

request.description: Returns all messages for a conversation ordered by id ASC. Returns an empty `messages` array for unknown conversation IDs.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description: Returns all registered prompt personas from the database. Each prompt may include a `model` field (null if using the global default).

response.name: 200 OK

request.description: Returns all models available in the static model registry. Use a `slug` value as `model_slug` in conversation requests to override the model for that request.

response.name: 200 OK
